debt_collection.name: Debt Recovery / Loan Notice
debt_collection.governing_law: SARFAESI Act 2002, RBI Fair Practices Code, Recovery of Debts and Bankruptcy Act 1993
key_rights: Right to receive 60-day notice before any action under SARFAESI Act
Right to file objection with the bank within 30 days of SARFAESI notice
Recovery agents cannot visit before 7am or after 7pm
Recovery agents cannot use abusive language or threaten family members
Right to approach Banking Ombudsman for free grievance redressal
Civil loan default cannot result in arrest — threatening arrest is illegal
Right to request a complete account statement showing all charges
common_violations: Threatening arrest or criminal action for civil loan default
Recovery agents contacting at odd hours (before 7am or after 7pm)
Not providing mandatory 60-day SARFAESI notice before taking action
Misrepresenting the outstanding amount or adding undisclosed charges
Contacting family members, employer, or neighbors about the debt
Using threatening or abusive language in written communication
debt_collection.forum: Banking Ombudsman or Debt Recovery Tribunal
debt_collection.template_response: I write with reference to your notice dated [DATE] regarding loan account [NUMBER]. I wish to bring to your attention that this notice does not comply with the mandatory 60-day period required under the SARFAESI Act 2002. I also note that threatening criminal consequences for civil loan default violates the RBI Fair Practices Code. I request a complete statement of account including all principal, interest, and charges. All further communication must be in writing. I reserve my right to approach the Banking Ombudsman if this matter is not resolved appropriately.

eviction_notice.name: Eviction / Vacation Notice
eviction_notice.governing_law: Transfer of Property Act 1882, State Rent Control Acts, Model Tenancy Act 2021
key_rights: Landlord must give at least 15 days notice for month-to-month tenancy
Cannot be evicted without a court order in most states
Right to challenge notice in Rent Control Court
Landlord cannot cut off electricity, water, or access to force eviction
Right to receive security deposit back within reasonable time after vacating
common_violations: Cutting off electricity or water supply to force vacating
Locking out tenant without a court order
Notice period shorter than legally required
Attempting eviction without registering rental agreement
eviction_notice.forum: Rent Control Court or Civil Court
eviction_notice.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of your notice dated [DATE]. I wish to state that I contest this notice as it does not comply with the requirements under the applicable Rent Control Act. I request you to refrain from any action that interferes with my peaceful possession of the premises. Any unlawful eviction attempt will be resisted through appropriate legal channels.

court_summons.name: Court Summons / Legal Notice
court_summons.governing_law: Code of Civil Procedure 1908, Legal Services Authorities Act 1987
key_rights: Right to file written reply within 30 days of receiving summons
Right to legal representation — free legal aid available if income below threshold
Ignoring a summons results in ex-parte order against you
Right to inspect court documents related to your case
Right to free legal aid via District Legal Services Authority
common_violations: Summons not properly served as per CPC requirements
Insufficient time given for response
Summons from wrong court without jurisdiction
court_summons.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of the summons in Case No. [NUMBER]. I intend to file my written statement within the time granted by the court. I request reasonable time to engage legal counsel.

employment_termination.name: Termination / Retrenchment Letter
employment_termination.governing_law: Industrial Disputes Act 1947, Shops and Establishments Act, Payment of Gratuity Act 1972
key_rights: One month notice or pay in lieu for employees with over 1 year of service
Retrenchment compensation of 15 days wages per completed year of service
Right to challenge wrongful termination at Labour Court within 3 years
Gratuity entitlement after 5 years of continuous service
Right to provident fund settlement and final salary within stipulated time
common_violations: Termination without required notice period or pay in lieu
Non-payment of gratuity after 5 years of service
Termination for trade union activity or protected reasons
Not following termination procedure under Standing Orders
employment_termination.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of the termination letter dated [DATE]. I wish to state that I have completed [X] years of service and am entitled to [notice pay/gratuity/retrenchment compensation] as per applicable law. I request a complete and proper settlement of all dues within the statutory period.

consumer_notice.name: Consumer / Company Notice
consumer_notice.governing_law: Consumer Protection Act 2019, Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules 2020
key_rights: Right to file complaint at District Consumer Commission for claims up to ₹50 lakh
State Commission handles claims ₹50 lakh to ₹2 crore
National Commission for claims above ₹2 crore
National Consumer Helpline: 1800-11-4000 (free)
Right to claim compensation for deficiency of service and mental harassment
common_violations: Refusing refund for defective product
Not honoring warranty or guarantee claims
Misrepresentation in advertising or at point of sale
Unfair trade practices or restrictive trade practices
consumer_notice.template_response: I write to formally notify you that I am a consumer who purchased [PRODUCT/SERVICE] on [DATE] for [AMOUNT]. The [product/service] has [describe defect/deficiency]. I request you to remedy this within 15 days failing which I will file a complaint before the District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission under the Consumer Protection Act 2019.
